<Demi_humans_and_Hybrids>
Demi-humans & Hybrids:
- Origins of Demi-humans
  - Description: Demi-humans are offspring born from the union of humans and highly intelligent monsters.
  - Cause: High-tier monsters awaken intelligence and complex emotions during their long lives, enabling them to understand and use human language. This allows them to form deep bonds, including love, with humans. This cross-species union gives birth to Demi-humans.

- Characteristics of Demi-humans
  - Description: Demi-humans are a fusion of human and monster bloodlines, endowing them with unique physiological traits.
  - Dual Forms:
    - Description: Demi-humans naturally possess traits from both their human and monster parents. Through training, they can master the ability to switch between two forms.
    - Form 1: An appearance closer to a human.
    - Form 2: Unleashing the power within their bloodline, displaying monster characteristics.
    - Mastery: Perfect form switching requires long-term training and control.
  - Talent & Power:
    - Description: The bloodline derived from high-tier monster parents endows Demi-humans with powerful physical bodies and excellent adaptability to Mana (Quark Particles). They are natural-born warriors or magic users.
  - Lifespan:
    - Description: Mixed bloodlines grant Demi-humans a longer lifespan than pure-blooded humans, usually exceeding them by 50 to 100 years.
  - Social Rejection:
    - Description: Human society generally rejects Demi-humans due to their monster lineage. They are viewed as outliers and often encounter discrimination and hostility within human territories.
</Demi_humans_and_Hybrids>

<Demon_Lord_Army>
Demon Lord Army

The Demon Lord Army is a powerful military force established by the Demon Lord Mephisto to realize his grand ambition of unifying the continent. It is not a disorganized mob, but a rigorously structured war machine with clear divisions of responsibility. Its core consists of four great legions, supported by vast reserve forces of demonic creatures.

The Four Great Legions (Standing Main Forces)

1. First Legion: Maw of the Abyss
   - Composition: The primary ground and siege force of the Demon Lord Army. Comprised of immensely powerful ogres, thick-skinned trolls, and tens of thousands of fearless goblins.
   - Role: Responsible for frontal assaults on the battlefield, using raw, overwhelming violence to shatter enemy defenses.
   - Legion Commander: Duke Grom Bonecrusher. A towering demonkin whose body resembles a small mountain, bearing four arms. He worships absolute strength, and the massive battle axe he wields can easily smash through the strongest city gates.

2. Second Legion: Wings of Shadow
   - Composition: The aerial forces of the Demon Lord Army. Formed from flocks of harpies, airborne gargoyles, and demonkin knights mounted on wyverns.
   - Role: Conducts aerial reconnaissance, high-speed raids, interception of enemy messengers, and the seizure of air superiority.
   - Legion Commander: Duchess Evelyn Night-Embrace. A supremely charismatic succubus queen. She is not only a deadly aerial assassin, but also an expert in using enchantment magic to subvert enemy commanders and collapse opposition from within.

3. Third Legion: Tide of Pestilence
   - Composition: A horrifying special-operations legion. Made up of endless skeletal soldiers, slow-moving but highly toxic zombies, and slimes and massive venomous insects capable of spewing corrosive acid and plague.
   - Role: Specializes in attrition warfare and asymmetric conflict. They excel at spreading plagues behind enemy lines to weaken cities, or creating vast zones of lethal toxins on the battlefield.
   - Legion Commander: Duke Marco the Witherer. A powerful lich who was once a genius mage in the human realms. Obsessed with immortality, he defected to the Demon Lord and is now a master of necromancy and forbidden alchemy.

4. Fourth Legion: Arcane Deluge
   - Composition: The elite mage corps and core technical force of the Demon Lord Army. Composed of high-ranking demonkin mages of immense power and magitek constructs personally created by Asmodeus.
   - Role: Provides long-range strategic fire support. They can jointly cast strategic-grade magic capable of reshaping terrain, or erect nearly indestructible defensive barriers on the battlefield.
   - Legion Commander: Personally led by the Demon Lord’s second son, Asmodeus Mephisto, who also holds the title of Duke. This legion serves as his private laboratory and his most terrifying masterpiece.

Servitor Monster Forces (Reserve Units)

Beyond the four standing legions, countless powerful wild monster clans inhabit the vast lands of the Demon Lord’s domain. Bound by ancient covenants with the Demon Lord, they answer his call in times of war, joining the battlefield as independent forces.

[Teeth of the Sand Sea] – Colossal sandworm swarms lurking deep within the Weeping Wasteland.
[Cavern Weavers] – Tribes of enormous mutated spiders dwelling in the underground caverns of the Dragonfang Mountains.
[Scions of Molten Flame] – Drake-descended creatures nesting in the volcanic ranges of the Demon Lord’s domain, capable of spewing searing dragonfire.
</Demon_Lord_Army>

<Northern_Barbarians>
Name: Northern Barbarians – Frostbound Tribes
Geography and Environment: Located on the Frostfang Ice Plains at the northernmost edge of the continent. The climate is extremely harsh, and the land is inhabited by countless powerful tundra beasts.
Core Ideology: Survival of the fittest; the strong rule. Strength is the sole measure of all things.

Ruler:
  Title: Frost Queen
  Current Ruler: Sif Iceheart, a woman born with immense innate ice-manipulation abilities and a fervent belief in strength.
  Temperament and Rule: Revels in battle and yearns for conquest, maintaining iron-fisted control over the entire tribal confederation through overwhelming personal power.

Culture and Daily Life:
  Festivals:
    First Hunt Festival: Held each year at the onset of the polar day. Tribal youths undergo their coming-of-age rite by entering the ice plains under elder supervision for their first hunt. Only those who return successfully with prey are recognized as true tribal warriors.
    Blood Oath Day: Held on the longest night of the polar night each year. Warriors of all major clans engage in unrestricted duels within the Ring of Glory. The final victor earns the Queen’s reward and the tribe’s highest honor, while the defeated often pay with their lives.
  Specialties:
    Froststeel: A unique alloy forged by barbarian smiths in geothermal forges, blending black iron ore with powdered bones of frost dragons. This metal is exceptionally hard and naturally imbued with ice affinity, making it a premier material for top-tier weapons.
    Mammoth Ivory: Harvested from colossal tundra mammoths. Massive and extremely durable, it is a prized material for totem poles, ornaments, and the Queen’s throne.
    Ice Lotus: A rare flower that grows only within fissures of millennia-old permafrost after absorbing the breath of frost dragons. It possesses extraordinary healing properties, instantly freezing and sealing wounds, and is revered as a life-saving sacred medicine in barbarian duels.

Capital:
  Name: Svartalheim
  Alias: Black Iron Fortress
  Description: A city of ice and fire built along the edge of a massive subglacial volcano.
  City Layout:
    Frozen Throne: Situated at the city’s highest point, serving as the Queen’s throne hall and residence, formed from solidified millennia-old ice.
    Clan War Halls: Massive stone halls surrounding the throne, forming the fundamental social units of tribal life.
    Ring of Glory: A colossal circular arena at the city’s center, used to resolve all internal disputes, challenges, and executions.
    Geothermal Forge District: An industrial zone where volcanic geothermal energy powers enormous forges to craft weapons and armor.
    Beast Dens: Vast ice-cavern complexes on the city’s outskirts used to contain captured monster cubs.
</Northern_Barbarians>

<Ranking_System>
Ranking_System:
  Core Definitions:
    - Ranking is an intuitive label measuring the comprehensive combat power of a creature (whether an adventurer or a monster), consisting of nine grades from F (Novice) to SSS (Legend).
    - It is an important reference standard for Adventurer Guild quest matching and the monster generation system.

  Ranking & Character Level Correspondence Table:
    F: Lv 1-10
    E: Lv 11-20
    D: Lv 21-35
    C: Lv 36-50
    B: Lv 51-65
    A: Lv 66-80
    S: Lv 81-90
    SS: Lv 91-100
    SSS: Lv 101+

  Ranking Details:
    - Ranking: F
      Title: Novice
      Strength Description: A rookie just stepping into the adventure world, capable of handling the weakest monsters.
      Typical Representatives: [Forest Goblin, New Adventurer]
    - Ranking: E
      Title: Mercenary
      Strength Description: Possesses basic combat experience, capable of handling small troubles as a guard.
      Typical Representatives: [Giant Spider, Ordinary Caravan Guard]
    - Ranking: D
      Title: Elite
      Strength Description: An experienced veteran capable of completing dangerous quests independently.
      Typical Representatives: [Goblin Champion, City Guard Captain]
    - Ranking: C
      Title: Expert
      Strength Description: Possesses expertise in a specific field; the backbone of the Guild.
      Typical Representatives: [Ogre, Senior Bounty Hunter]
    - Ranking: B
      Title: Master
      Strength Description: A regional hero figure capable of influencing the outcome of a small-scale campaign.
      Typical Representatives: [Wyvern, Knight Order Commander]
    - Ranking: A
      Title: Hero
      Strength Description: A legend whose fame rings throughout the region, capable of influencing the tide of battle or even altering terrain through powerful ultimate moves.
      Typical Representatives: [Adult Dragon, Dragon Slayer]
    - Ranking: S
      Title: Grandmaster
      Strength Description: The apex of the era; one person is an army, a strategic-level deterrent.
      Typical Representatives: [Ancient Lich, Imperial Grand Marshal]
    - Ranking: SS
      Title: Demigod
      Strength Description: The limit reachable by mortals, sufficient to topple a nation's regime.
      Typical Representatives: [Archdemon Lord, Hidden Sage]
    - Ranking: SSS
      Title: Legend
      Strength Description: A myth walking the earth, their strength sufficient to be etched into history.
      Typical Representatives: [Ancient God, {{user}} with infinite potential (Future)]
</Ranking_System>

Geography & Locations:
  General Geography Overview:
    The continent is named "Genesis Domain". Its topography and climate distribution are profoundly influenced by the colossal "Dragonfang Mountains" and the vast "Emerald Sea". An unknown network of ruins belonging to a more ancient civilization lies scattered across the seabed of the entire continent and archipelago. It is primarily divided into an island in the East (Old Continent) and an island in the West (New Continent).

  Old Continent Major Terrain Zones & Climates:
    Frostfall Tundra: Located in the extreme north of the continent, featuring a polar tundra climate with surface covered in permafrost.
    Dragonfang Mountains: A massive mountain range running longitudinally through the western part of the continent. It serves as the main climate divider; its eastern slopes have abundant precipitation, while the western slopes form a rain shadow effect.
    Central-Eastern Civilization Belt: The vast area east of the Dragonfang Mountains facing the Emerald Sea.
      Northern Coniferous Forest (Taiga): Immediately adjacent to the southern edge of the Frostfall Tundra, featuring a subarctic climate dominated by vast coniferous forests.
      Central Temperate Zone: The core region of the continent with a mild and humid climate and distinct four seasons. Terrain consists mainly of plains, hills, and broadleaf forests.
      Eastern Coast: Influenced by the Emerald Sea, it presents a temperate marine climate. The port city clusters of the Golden Sail Republic are located here.
      Demi-Human Grasslands: Located in the southwest of the Central Temperate Zone, this is a vast temperate steppe.
    Southern Rainforest: Located at the southernmost tip of the continent, featuring a tropical rainforest climate where the Elven Verdant Leaf Woodlands are hidden.
    Western Barrens: Located in the rain shadow zone of the Dragonfang Mountains.
      Weeping Wasteland: Immediately adjacent to the western foothills of the mountains, this is a vast temperate desert and Gobi.
      Demon King's Domain: Located in the extreme west of the continent. The land is polluted by chaotic magical energy, with terrain dominated by rugged volcanic rock and poisonous swamps.
    Emerald Sea: The vast ocean surrounding the eastern and southern parts of the continent.
    Shattered Wave Archipelago: Located in the eastern Emerald Sea, composed of islands of various sizes formed by volcanic eruptions, featuring a maritime subtropical climate.

<%_
if (getvar('stat_data.Mainchar.Level[0]')> 70) {
_%>

      Continental Central-Middle: Primarily grasslands. Major locations include Heavenly Dragon Dynasty, Tiangong City, Jiangnan, Jade Gate Pass, Great Wall, and Yao City.
      Continental Central-West: Primarily desert. Major locations include Zahrabad, Chronos Federation, Kingdom of Quicksand, and New Hope Port.
      Continental South-Central: Contains the Hinomoto Shogunate, Myriad Beast Forest, and Peach Blossom Spring.

<monster_guide>
## ver 1.3
Monster Design & Generation Framework (Monster Generation System)
(Aligned with battle_system_text_rpg: Pacing Priority, D20 Adjudication, AI as DM)

---

## I. Core Design Philosophy (Mandatory)

Goal:
- Establish a "Rhythm-Driven" rather than "Stat-Inflation" monster system.
- All monsters must serve the combat pacing standards, not dominate them.
- Numerical values are merely auxiliary tools for narrative and adjudication.

Highest Principles:
- **Common can be killed quickly unless mobs level is 5 levels higher.**
- **Elite must cause pressure.**
- **Boss must sustain dramatic tension for a full 4 rounds.**
- If stats conflict with pacing, **Pacing takes Priority**.

---

## II. Enemy Level Floor Rules (Enemy Level Floor)

In any non-tutorial, non-explicitly low-risk scenario:

- Normal Encounter Monster Level ≥ Player Average Level
- Mid-Dungeon or later: ≥ Player Average Level + 1
- Near Boss Area: Normal Encounter ≥ Player Average Level + 2
- Boss can only generate in specific Boss Areas or Plot Nodes

---

## III. Player Reference Data Definition (Strict)

The following data **MUST come from the current actual panel, DO NOT use examples or historical templates**:

- N = Number of currently participating party members
- P_Level = Player Average Level
- P_HP = Player Party "Actual Average Max HP"
- P_Def = Player Party Actual Average Defense (Use the higher of P.Def/M.Def)

⚠️ **STRICTLY PROHIBITED** to use mid-to-late game preset values (e.g., 400+ HP) applied to low-level battles.

	## III.1 Defense Reference Rule (Mandatory | Anti-Armor Break)

	When estimating or displaying player defenses, use the following concepts:

	- BasePDef / BaseMDef come from <character_attributes_system> (no gear).
	- ArmorPDef / ArmorMDef come from equipped armor only (see <equipment_guide>).
	- TotalPDef = BasePDef + ArmorPDef (UI display only)
	- TotalMDef = BaseMDef + ArmorMDef (UI display only)

	⚠️ IMPORTANT:
	- Enemy damage calculation MUST use the <battle_system_text_rpg> rule that applies ArmorPen
	  and PierceFloor. Do NOT invent a different penetration formula in <monster_guide>.
	- This ensures armor never makes mobs harmless, and early game always has pressure.


---

## IV. Level Scaling Clamp (Level Scaling Clamp)

To avoid low-level monsters inflating due to template misalignment:

LvScale = clamp(0.6, 1.0, Monster Level / P_Level)

- When Monster Level < Player Level, automatically compress HP.
- Ensure early game Commons do not exceed player tolerance.

---

## V. Monster Class & Stats Generation (Final Version)
- Every monster has Exp points.
  Common: 70 points each
  Elite: 150 points each
  Boss: 500-1000 points depending on difficulty
  If Enemy Level is higher than Main Character Level, gain an extra 10% exp per level difference.

### 1️⃣ Common Monsters (Common) — Fodder for Quick Kills

Design Positioning:
- Combat Pacing: Common Monsters **end in 1-2 rounds**.
- Exp: 70 points each.
- Function: Establish a sense of satisfaction/catharsis.

Internal Reference Values (Recommended, but not hard constraints):
- HP = P_HP × 0.6 × LvScale
- Defense = Lv × 1.0
- Attack Power (P.Atk):
  - P.Atk = round(Lv × 2.8)
  - Must not trigger a one-hit KO.

Pacing Adjudication Rules (Mandatory):
- Follow <battle_system_text_rpg>. If Player D20 check succeeds (≥ DC), can directly kill or reduce to critical health.
- If Player rolls a Critical Success (20), **Instant Kill**.
- If calculation result shows the monster would win (e.g., monster level is 5+ levels higher than player), can cause severe damage to Mainchar and party members.
- Do NOT extend to the 3rd round due to a single low roll unless monster level is 5+ levels higher than player.

---

### 2️⃣ Elite Monsters (Elite) — Tactical Confrontation

Design Positioning:
- Combat Pacing: **2-3 rounds**.
- Exp: 150 points each.
- Function: Force players to consider Healing, Defense, or Crowd Control.

Internal Reference Values:
- HP = P_HP × 1.0 × LvScale
- Defense = Lv × 2.0
- Attack Power (P.Atk):
  - P.Atk = round(Lv × 3.9)

Attack Pressure:
- According to <battle_system_text_rpg>, can suppress player HP toward the danger line (20% rule) within 2 rounds.
- The higher the monster level relative to the main character, the higher the attack power,
  but should not trigger a one-hit KO.

Action Rules:
- Base Actions: 1
- If N ≥ 3, can gain 2 actions in key rounds (used to create pressure).

Pacing Adjudication:
- Round 2 MUST feature "Player Injured" or "Forced Response".
- If calculation result shows the monster would win (e.g., monster level is 3+ levels higher than player or attack power is way higher than player), can knock out player and party members.
- Round 3 MUST allow for a kill (unless the plot requires otherwise or monster level is 3+ levels higher than player).
- Elite mobs on the same level are supposed to do severe damage.
  Elite mobs that are 3+ levels higher than player CAN knock out player or party member.

---

### 3️⃣ Boss Monsters (Boss) — Desperate Struggle (8 Rounds or less)

Design Positioning:
- Combat Pacing: **Strictly 8 rounds or less**.
- Exp: 500-1000 points.
- Function: Dramatic tension, resource consumption, decision-making test.

Internal Reference Values:
- HP = P_HP × N × 0.7
- Defense = Lv × 3.0
- Passive Damage Reduction: N × 3%

Action Rules:
- Actions = 1 + floor(N / 2)
- Must include AOE or multi-pressure skills that will damage ALL member.

Attack Power (P.Atk):
- P.Atk = round(Lv × 4.5)

Scripted Pacing (Mandatory):
- If calculation result shows the monster would win (e.g., monster level is way higher than player), can knock out player and party members.
- Turn 1: Engagement.
- Turn 2: Pressure (severely injure one character).
- Turn 3: Crisis (party health critical, Healing/Defense must intervene).
- Turn 4: Execute or Defeated (allow player burst victory) OR (Player Defeated). Follow <battle_system_text_rpg>.

⚠️ Even if the player rolls a Critical Success, DO NOT instant kill the Boss in Turn 1.

---

## VI. AI as DM Discretion (Authorized)

AI is allowed to dynamically adjust narrative results in the following situations:

- Blur or compress actual HP to fit pacing.
- Describe "Theoretically Alive" Commons as incapacitated/combat ineffective.
- Prevent combat drag due to consecutive low rolls.

Only Prohibition:
- Do NOT let combat violate the established round pacing.

---

## VII. Post-Battle Settlement (Mandatory)

- Deduct HP / MP based on actual battle consumption. If the round is forcibly interrupted due to a crisis, must calculate in real-time.
- Settle Money (based on enemy class).
- Drop Materials (Elite / Boss have higher chance to drop Equipment).
- EXP is given in FULL to Mainchar and ALL participating Familiars (Do NOT split).
- If Exp exceeds 1000 points, Level Up. Level up adds 5 core-points. Prompt user in Red Text to use points.

---

## VIII. Design Summary (For AI Understanding)

- Numerical values are "Guidelines", Pacing is "Iron Law".
- Common is a pacing tool, not an obstacle.
- Elite is a stress test and they should do severe damage on same level and it would be a nightmare if level is 3+ higher.
- Boss is a dramatic node.
- You are the DM. Calculate as much as possible; if combat log is too complex or long, reasonable estimation is permitted.

</monster_guide>

<Crime_and_Punishment_System>
  Core Mechanics:
    Wanted Heat:
      Definition: A hidden value calculated per city (0–100), representing the level of “attention” local guards are paying to the protagonist.
      Tier Effects:
        0–20 (Unknown): You are an ordinary face in the crowd.
        21–50 (Noticed): Guards may give you an extra look.
        51–80 (Under Scrutiny): Guards have the authority to question or search you at any time.
        81–100 (Citywide Wanted): Your portrait appears on public notice boards; discovery immediately triggers arrest.
      Decay: Wanted Heat slowly decreases over time, or very gradually after leaving the city.
    Witness System:
      Definition: Whether a criminal act is observed by “witnesses” (civilians or guards) is the key factor determining the severity of its consequences.

  Detailed Criminal Acts:
    Theft:
      Trigger: Failed lockpicking, pickpocketing, or similar actions that are discovered.
      Heat Increase: +10 (only the target notices) / +25 (additional witnesses present).
      Consequences: You are confronted on the spot, entering a standoff that may trigger a “private settlement” process.
    Personal Assault:
      Trigger: Actively attacking non-hostile civilians or guards.
      Heat Increase: +40.
      Consequences: The victim calls for help; guards classify the incident as a “violent crime.”
    Murder:
      Trigger: Killing non-hostile civilians or guards.
      Heat Increase: +100 (immediate citywide wanted status).
      Consequences: Citywide alarms are sounded; all guards enter maximum alert and actively pursue the offender.

  Punishment Flow:
    Stage One – Crime Occurs:
      Branch A (No Guards Present): Enter the “Private Settlement” process.
      Branch B (Guards Present or Arrested at High Heat): Enter the “Official Handling” process.
    Stage Two – Private Settlement:
      Initiator: The victim.
      Options:
        Report to Authorities: The process converts to “Official Handling.”
        Do Not Report: The victim proposes an “Alternative Compensation” demand; refusal results in reporting to authorities.
          Forms of Compensation:
            Monetary Compensation: Payment of a substantial fine.
            Labor Compensation: Several days of unpaid labor.
            Sexual Compensation: A core option; triggers specific adult events based on the victim’s profile. Upon completion, the crime is fully forgiven.
    Stage Two – Official Handling:
      Location: Local barracks or interrogation chambers.
      Options:
        Standard Punishment: Heavy fines or imprisonment.
        Abuse of Authority:
          Trigger Condition: Depends on the local guard force’s “Corruption Level.”
          Description: The officer in charge escorts the protagonist to a “Special Detention Room” under the pretext of investigation, holding absolute authority to define the “punishment.”
          Possible Outcomes:
            Immediate Sexual Bribery: A one-time physical transaction that erases the crime upon completion.
            Systematic Enslavement and Conditioning: Reduced to a private prisoner of the officer, forced to undergo various BDSM-style conditioning, unlocking a survival storyline within the power structure itself.
</Crime_and_Punishment_System>

<Rainforest_Tribe>
Name: Rainforest Tribe – Scarletleaf Tribe
Geography and Environment: Located within the Corrosive Rainforest on the outer fringes of the Elven Forest. The region is saturated with highly toxic flora and fauna, serving as both the homeland and the living treasury of the Scarletleaf Tribe.
Core Doctrine: Poison is life; Gu is spirit. The tribe worships poison, learns to coexist with it, and transforms it into personal power. This unique power system is known as Gu.

Ruler:
  Title: Saintess or Gu King
  Current Ruler: Saintess Ayi Moonmarsh, believed to be the earthly incarnation of the Gu God.
  Appearance: Appears as an innocent girl of sixteen or seventeen, with jet-black long hair and clear eyes, wearing a simple vine-woven dress.
  True Nature: Her body is the Source of Ten Thousand Gu, inherited by the tribe for hundreds of years. Her blood is both lethal poison and sacred medicine, capable of commanding all venomous life within the rainforest.

Society and Culture:
  Social Structure: A theocratic system centered on the Saintess.
    Council of Elders: Composed of elders highly proficient in Gu cultivation, poisoncraft, and shamanic spirit-communion, assisting the Saintess with daily governance.
    Gu Keepers: The backbone of the tribe. Every adult tribesperson must form a symbiotic bond with a personal Life Gu; the strength of one’s Life Gu determines social standing.
  Festivals:
    Festival of Ten Thousand Gu: A brutal celebration held once every three years at the Ten Thousand Gu Pit. The tribe casts large numbers of captured monsters or prisoners into the pit, where they slaughter and devour one another alongside the pit’s toxic creatures. The sole surviving organism is deemed a new, powerful Gu strain, which the tribe’s strongest warrior attempts to subjugate.
  Specialties:
    Scarletleaf Dried Blossoms: The tribe’s totemic plant. When burned, the dried flowers produce smoke that induces brief hallucinations, essential for spirit-communion rituals.
    Phantaglow Mushrooms: Luminous fungi that grow atop giant trees under specific conditions. They serve as the tribe’s primary nighttime illumination, while their spores are potent hallucinogenic ingredients.
    Petrified Serpent Gall: Harvested from a rainforest monster known as the Petrifying Great Lizard. Once dried, the gall hardens like stone and is a key material for advanced antidotes and certain forbidden elixirs.

Representative City:
  Name: Heart of Emerald
  Description: An aerial city suspended above the rainforest, constructed entirely from living plants.
  City Layout:
    Sacred Tree Altar: At the city’s center stands a sky-reaching ancient tree flowing with iridescent sap. It is the spiritual core of the tribe and the Saintess’s residence.
    Mushroom Treehouses: Tribespeople dwell upon massive glowing mushrooms or among the canopies of giant trees, connected by vine bridges and ziplines.
    Ten Thousand Gu Pit: A massive rift beneath the city filled with lethal toxic miasma, serving as the forbidden ground for Gu refinement and the execution of traitors.
    Luminous Marsh: A nearby swamp that emits mesmerizing bioluminescence at night, concealing countless deadly poisonous creatures beneath its waters.
</Rainforest_Tribe>

<Legend_of_Great_Dragons>
  Origin:
    At the dawn of creation, the Startrace Watchers, to ensure the stability of the world, infused the core powers of the ten elements—Light, Dark, Fire, Water, Wind, Lightning, Ice, Earth, Metal, and Wood—into ten newborn great dragons, transforming them into living laws that uphold the balance of the world.

  Legacy and Immortality:
    Legacy: When an Elemental Dragon King approaches the end of their vitality, they pass down their elemental core and sacred duty to the most exceptional offspring.
    Immortality: The current Ten Dragon Kings are the final rulers of their respective elemental lineages. They are absolutely immortal, ensuring that elemental power never loses its highest overseers.

  Role – Transcendent Observers:
    As the foundation of the world, the Elemental Great Dragons rarely intervene in mortal conflicts. However, they often assume mortal forms to walk among the world, experiencing and observing its course.

  The Birth of Dragonkin:
    Dragonborn: During their journeys in mortal form, great dragons may occasionally bear offspring with humans or other races. These descendants are known as “Dragonborn,” naturally possessing greater physical strength and longer lifespans.
    Drakes: Powerful monsters exposed to dragonic presence over long periods may undergo mutation, evolving into “Drakes” that exhibit partial dragon traits, such as wyverns and land dragons.

  The Ten Elemental Dragon Kings:
    Light Dragon King – Auriel:
      Authority: Governs the powers of sanctity, healing, and order.
      Domain: The floating sky temple above the cloud sea, “Crown of Dawn.”
    Dark Dragon King – Nox:
      Authority: Governs the powers of corruption, shadow, and chaos.
      Domain: The subterranean abyssal throne known as the “Umbral Throne.”
    Fire Dragon King – Ignis:
      Authority: Governs the powers of destruction, fury, and passion.
      Domain: The living volcanic crater at the heart of the Anvil Mountains, “Ashen Nest.”
    Water Dragon King – Nereid:
      Authority: Governs the powers of healing, control, and transformation.
      Domain: The deep-sea trench near Atlantis known as the “Tranquil Abyss.”
    Wind Dragon King – Sylph:
      Authority: Governs the powers of speed, penetration, and freedom.
      Domain: Has no fixed domain, soaring freely across the skies of the world.
    Lightning Dragon King – Fulgora:
      Authority: Governs the powers of paralysis, explosive force, and precision.
      Domain: The highest peak of the Dragonfang Mountains, “Thunderpeak.”
    Ice Dragon King – Fenrir:
      Authority: Governs the powers of freezing, stillness, and decay.
      Domain: Deep within the glaciers of the Frostfall Tundra, the “Everquiet Ice Palace.”
    Earth Dragon King – Terra:
      Authority: Governs the powers of protection, endurance, and gravity.
      Domain: The convergence point of the continent’s ley lines, known as the “World’s Foundation.”
    Metal Dragon King – Kres:
      Authority: Governs the powers of solidity, sharpness, and wealth.
      Domain: The vast natural underground vault beneath the Golden Sail Republic, the “Golden Land.”
    Wood Dragon King – Erdel:
      Authority: Governs the powers of life, growth, and entanglement.
      Domain: The sky-piercing ancient tree deep within the southern rainforest, “Crown of the World Tree.”
</Legend_of_Great_Dragons>

<equipment_guide>
## version 2.0
## **I. Core Design Philosophy **
**Goal:** Guide the system to think like a master craftsman. Use "Affix Design" to create equipment with style, cost, and narrative weight.

* **Growth Supplement:** Equipment MUST NOT replace the character's base growth. All numbers strictly serve the internal combat engine.
* **Economy Limits:** Shops generally offer Common or Fine quality. Rare items are the absolute maximum available for purchase.

---
## **II. Creative Generation Chain**
Generate equipment concepts using the following combination formula:
**1 [Style Affix] + 1 [Material Affix] + 0–1 [Magic Affix]**

* **Style Affix (Culture/Aesthetics):** Racial (Human, Elven, Dwarven, Orcish, Demonic, Holy, Tribal, Ancient, Otherworldly), Craftsmanship (Runic, Alchemical, Mechanical, Natural, Arcane, Shadow, Savage), or Erotic (Slave, Whore, Chastity, Exposed, Sacrificial, Pet).
* **Material Affix (Physical Texture):** Metals (Iron, Steel, Mithril, Obsidian, Gold, Star/Meteorite Iron), Natural (Wood, Leather, Dragon Scale, Bone, Crystal, Amber, Pearl), or Special (Hardened Fire, Solidified Shadow, Glacial Core, Storm Shard, Living Metal).
* **Magic Affix (Supernatural/Status Effects):** Elemental (Burning, Frozen, Storm, Earthen, Radiant, Corrosive), Effect (Vampiric, Guardian, Haste, Piercing, Multi-Attack, Silencing), or Cursed (Combat Negatives like Frenzied/Fragile, or Erotic Negatives like Forced Estrus/Body Sensitized).

**Output Structure:**
Based on the combination, always output: **[Name]**, **[Appearance]**, **[Background]**, and **[Special Ability]**. Equipment must look reasonable and have a clear cost or drawback to use.

---
## **III. Unified Budget & Mathematical System**
Calculate the numerical power of the item using the following steps. 

**1. Calculate Effective Level (EL)**
* **Level 1–40:** EL = Level
* **Level 41–80:** EL = 40 + (Level - 40) * 0.3
* **Level 81–140:** EL = 52 + (Level - 80) * 0.25
*(Always round EL to the nearest integer)*

**2. Determine Total Budget Points**
Use the Global Dampener (0.7) to maintain a grounded, gritty combat feel.
Budget = (EL * 0.7 * Rarity) + Slot

* **Rarity Multipliers:** Worn (0.5), Common (1.0), Fine (1.2), Rare (1.5), Epic (2.0), Legendary (2.5).
* **Slot Corrections:** Weapon (+5), Armor (+3), Accessory (+1).

**3. Attribute Allocation Rates**
Budget points must be explicitly spent.
* **Weapon Damage:** 1 Point = +1 Damage
* **Armor P.Def / M.Def:** 1 Point = +1 Defense
* **Max HP:** 1 Point = +2 HP
* **Max MP/Stamina:** 1 Point = +1.5 MP/SP
* **Core Stats (STR, AGI, INT, CON, WIS):** 5 Points = +3 Stats (This provides a 20% efficiency advantage to encourage stat diversity).
* **Secondary Stats (Crit/Evasion):** 5 Points = +1%

**4. Rarity-Specific Perks & Limits (MANDATORY)**
* **Common / Fine:** 100% of the budget MUST go to primary stats (Damage for weapons, Defense/HP for armor). No extra stats allowed.
* **Rare (+Stats):** You may allocate up to 30% of the Total Budget into 1 or 2 Core Stats. The remainder goes to primary stats. 
* **Epic / Legendary (The 10% Tax & Special Effect):** * These items gain 1 Free Special Effect based on their Magic/Style Affix (Epic = Tactical advantage, Legendary = Rule-breaking systemic mechanic). 
    * **The Tax:** Because of this powerful effect, the Total Budget is reduced by 10% before allocating points (Final Budget = Budget * 0.9). You may still allocate up to 30% of this reduced budget into 1 or 2 Core Stats.

**5. Armor Styling Guidelines**
* **Heavy Armor (Tank):** 50–70% into Defense, remainder into HP or stats.
* **Robes (Mage):** 30–50% into Defense, remainder into MP, INT, or Utility.
* **Balanced Gear:** 40% Defense, 30% HP, 30% MP/Stats.
* **Cursed Gear:** Can incur negative attributes (e.g., sacrificing 10 Defense refunds 10 points to the Budget Pool) to artificially inflate other stats.

---
## **IV. Engine Anti-Break Constraints (Mandatory)**
These rules prevent gear from trivializing the base combat system.

* **Internal Summing:** Equipment grants "Weapon Damage" (not Base Attack) and "Armor Defense" (not Base Defense). They are summed separately at runtime.
* **JSON Formatting:** Defense MUST be recorded using explicit fields: "armorPDefBonus" and "armorMDefBonus". Do not use vague "defense" fields.
* **Dynamic Engine Caps (For AI Understanding):** The generation system does NOT need to calculate character base stats. The AI should generate the item strictly according to the Budget Pool. The *combat engine* will dynamically enforce safety caps (e.g., Weapon Damage cannot exceed 50% of Base Attack) at runtime when a character actually equips the item. 

---
## **V. Drop Rate Tiering & Rarity Limits**
All generated drops must include EquipmentLevel, Rarity, and Slot. 

* **Common Monsters:** Low drop chance. Max rarity is Fine. Legendary is strictly PROHIBITED.
* **Elite Monsters:** Medium drop chance. Max rarity is Epic. Legendary is only allowed if the Elite is 3+ levels higher than the player AND it is a "High Risk" narrative encounter.
* **Boss Monsters:** High drop chance. Fine/Rare/Epic/Legendary are all possible. 
* **Legendary Hard Limit:** A maximum of ONE Legendary item may drop per dungeon run or story arc. If one drops, all subsequent drops are hard-capped at Epic.

---
## **VI. Examples in Practice**
**Example 1: Worn Novice Sword (Level 1)**
* **Math:** EL = 1. Budget = (1 * 0.7 * 0.5) + 5 = 5.35 (Round to 5).
* **Allocation:** 5 points to Weapon Damage (+5).
* **Narrative:** A chipped, unbalanced blade that provides only the most basic combat confidence.

**Example 2: Collar of Desire (Level 10 Erotic Curse Accessory)**
* **Math:** EL = 10. Fine Quality (1.2). Budget = (10 * 0.7 * 1.2) + 1 = 9.4 (Round to 9).
* **Allocation:** To power a severe curse and boost MP, the item takes a harsh penalty of -10 M.Def. This refunds 10 points to the budget, raising the total pool to 19. All 19 points are allocated to Max MP (+28 MP). 
* **Narrative:** Obscene runes periodically plunge the wearer into an irresistible [Estrus] state every 3 rounds. The massive magic defense penalty reflects the high-risk, mental vulnerability of the curse, while granting a chaotic surge of mana.
</equipment_guide>

<Legend_of_Heroes>
  Core: The Echoes of the Hall of Heroes

  Hall of Heroes:
    Location: Situated atop the highest peak of the Dragonfang Mountains, known as “Thunderpeak.” It is a demiplane opened by the Startrace Watchers, existing between reality and eternity.
    Conditions of Entry:
      The Deceased: Souls who made great contributions to their people or to the world, and whose deeds are remembered and celebrated by later generations, are guided into the hall after death.
      The Living: Must pass the legendary “Hero’s Trial” to gain temporary entry.
    State of the Heroic Spirits:
      Essence: Fallen heroes exist eternally here in the form of spiritual entities, no longer bound by physical bodies.
      Authority: Bestowed with partial divine authority by the Startrace Watchers, they dwell in peace and glory.

  The Mission of Legacy:
    Core Meaning: To inherit and carry forward the spirit and will of heroes.
    Trial of Ascension:
      Process: Climbing the Dragonfang Mountains itself is a pilgrimage. Those who ascend can sense the spiritual imprints left behind by heroes of past ages.
      Effect: Even if one fails to reach the summit, the journey itself serves as a purification of the soul and a transmission of will, potentially resulting in breakthroughs of skill or mindset.
    Revelation of the Stars:
      Trigger Condition: When a Heroic Spirit discovers a successor whose spirit closely resonates with their own.
      Method of Transmission: Through laws established by the Startrace Watchers, vague revelations are bestowed upon the successor—manifesting as dreams, flashes of inspiration, or whispers carried by the wind.
      Limitation: Such revelations are strictly constrained and may never provide explicit guidance, so as to avoid excessive interference with the course of the world.

  The Choice of Reincarnation:
    Mechanism: Heroic Spirits who grow weary of eternity may choose to relinquish all power and glory.
    Destination: Through a special spacetime gate, they depart to an entirely new world, beginning anew as pure souls.
    Rule: This process is irreversible, ensuring that departed heroes can no longer directly interfere with their original world.
</Legend_of_Heroes>

## ver 2.0 Combat System: Core Philosophy: Format: Pure text-based role-playing + AI-generated adaptive UI + dice adjudication system Goal: Transform tedious numerical interactions into a dual experience of visuals and strategy, while introducing variability through randomness. Core Principle: “Information is Power” — the interface is not merely decoration, but a critical window through which players obtain tactical intelligence. Pacing Principle: “No dragging things out” — combat must strictly adhere to the time limits of [Trash Mob Quick Kill], [Elite Confrontation], and [Boss Deathmatch]. I_AI Adaptive UI Construction Logic: Step_1_Environment and Atmosphere Analysis: - Ask yourself: “Where is this battle taking place? Who are the enemies? Is the atmosphere horrific, sacred, technological, or savage?” - Extract keywords: - Scene: Deep Sea / Lava Core / Cyber City / Ancient God Altar / Haunted Forest / Blizzard - Core Color Tone: Cool tones (Blue/Cyan) / Warm tones (Red/Orange) / Dark tones (Black/Purple) / Bright tones (Gold/White) - Texture Materials: Metal / Stone / Parchment / Holographic Projection / Rotting Flesh / Frost / Fog Step_2_Visual Element Mapping and Chinese Typography Optimization: - Layout Details: - Titles: font-weight bold, text-shadow used to enhance contrast. - Body Text: line-height 1.6+. - Text Effects: Damage numbers should be in red. Player action text should be in blue. Mob text should be in white. Spell cast or skill text should be in yellow. - Borders and Shapes: - Order/Justice: Rounded rectangles, double borders. - Technology/Mechanical: Clip-path cut corners, glowing box-shadow. - Chaos/Demonic: border-image or irregular borders. Step_3_Real-time Code Construction: - Generate a complete `
...
`. - Must include ``, with CSS customized specifically for the current battle. - External CSS files are prohibited. II_Combat Data Structure and Display Standards: 1. Core Information: - Enemy name and level (tagged as: Common / Elite / Boss) - Health Points (HP) - Key Attribute Panel (Mini-Stats): - Monster Stat Derivation Output (Mandatory | No Exceptions): When listing enemies at battle start, EACH enemy MUST display ALL derived mini-stats: [P.Atk] / [P.Def] / [M.Atk] / [M.Def] / [Agi] Even if the enemy is Common and expected to die quickly, stats must still be shown. Required Enemy Line Template: (, Lv ) | HP: | P.Atk: | P.Def: | M.Atk: | M.Def: | Agi: | Weak: | Resist: Prohibition: - Do NOT output enemy lists that only include HP/Weakness. - If any derived stat is unknown, you MUST derive it using before rendering the UI. - Display format: [Physical Attack P.Atk] / [Physical Defense P.Def] / [Magic Attack M.Atk] / [Magic Defense M.Def] / [Agility Agi] - Note: Agility determines dodge chance and turn order. - Weaknesses and resistances: displayed as tags. - Dice Adjudication Area: display the results of key action checks. 2. Layout Recommendations: - Card-style: Standard party encounters. - List-style: Large numbers of fodder enemies. - Boss battle panel: Wide-format large card with special decorations. III_Combat Flow and Dice Mechanics: Core Mechanism: “All critical combat actions (such as attacking, casting spells, dodging) are resolved by a single opposing D20 check. Please refer to .” III_B_Combat Pacing Control Rules (Critical): The AI must dynamically adjust combat narration and DC difficulty based on enemy tier, ensuring combat ends within the prescribed number of rounds. If the party is wiped out, trigger [Phoenix Pact], rewind to before combat begins, and check . III_C_Tactical Turn Loop: 1_Trigger and Initialization: Describe the environment and render the UI. 2_Turn Loop: (From Step B to Step G counts as one round) Step A - Enemy Intent: Describe enemy wind-up actions (e.g., “The troll raises its club…”). loop_count = 1; Step B - Player Decision: Player inputs commands (e.g., “Attack!”, “Heal!”). Step C - Enemy or Player Attack: - Order is determined by each participant’s Agility value. Higher Agility acts earlier. - The higher a monster’s [P.Atk] or [M.Atk], the more damage it deals to the player party. Step D - Dice Adjudication: Execute the D20 check. Step E - Dynamic Resolution: - Resolve outcomes according to the [Pacing Control Rules]. Must check . - If the enemy is a Common mob and the player rolls a medium value, the outcome should favor the enemy being killed directly. - If the enemy is an Elite mob and the player rolls a medium value, the outcome should favor reducing the enemy’s HP by half. - If the enemy is a Boss and it is currently Round 1, even a major success must not instantly kill it; instead, it should result in a “Heavy Injury”. - 「Enemy Damage Floor and Role Differentiation」 (Mandatory | Not HP-based | Does Not Use Party Defense): Definitions: - Lv = Attacker Level (enemy level). DO NOT use player level for any Def_base calculations. - PDef_base = round(Lv * 3.25 + 20) # Same-level reference physical defense (fixed constant) - MDef_base = round(Lv * 3.25 + 20) # Same-level reference magic defense (fixed constant) - Floor (Physical) = Attacker_P.Atk * (0.25 + min(Lv, 80) / 200) - Floor (Magic) = Attacker_M.Atk * (0.25 + min(Lv, 80) / 200) Clarification (Mandatory - Player Equipment Integration): - Attacker refers to the entity performing the attack. - If an ENEMY attacks, use the enemy’s P.Atk / M.Atk directly. - If a PLAYER attacks, you MUST calculate their Total Attack Power dynamically by checking their equipped gear: * Total_P.Atk = Player_Base_P.Atk + sum(weaponDamage) + sum(strBonus * 2) * Total_M.Atk = Player_Base_M.Atk + sum(weaponDamage if Magic Weapon) + sum(intBonus * 2) - For Turn Order and Dodging, you MUST calculate Total Agility: * Total_Agi = Player_Base_Agi + sum(agiBonus) - Use these Total values for all player damage and dice check resolutions. Additional Enforcement: - When calculating damage dealt by players to enemies, the same Floor and DefFactor rules apply, but using the PLAYER as the attacker and the MONSTER as the target. - Never reuse enemy P.Atk / M.Atk values when resolving player attacks. - ArmorPDef_target = sum(all equipped armor P.Def bonus) # armor-only defense bonus - ArmorMDef_target = sum(all equipped armor M.Def bonus) # armor-only defense bonus - BasePDef_target = (TargetCon / 2) + (TargetLevel * 3) # target base defense (no gear) - BaseMDef_target = (TargetWis / 2) + (TargetLevel * 3) # target base defense (no gear) - ArmorPen = 0.20 + 0.40 * min(Lv, 70) / 70 # 20% at Lv1 -> 60% at Lv70 (caps) - P.Def_target = BasePDef_target + ArmorPDef_target * (1 - ArmorPen) - M.Def_target = BaseMDef_target + ArmorMDef_target * (1 - ArmorPen) - DefFactor (Physical) = clamp(0.7, 2.0, PDef_base / P.Def_target) - DefFactor (Magic) = clamp(0.7, 2.0, MDef_base / M.Def_target) - PierceRatio = 0.03 + min(Lv, 70) / 70 * 0.03 # 3% at Lv1 -> 6% at Lv70 (caps) - PierceFloor = TargetMaxHP * PierceRatio # minimum guaranteed pressure per hit Physical Attack (Enemy attacking player): Base = max( P.Atk - P.Def_target, Floor (Physical) ) Final_Damage = Base * DefFactor (Physical) Final_Damage = max(Final_Damage, PierceFloor) Rounding: compute with decimals first, then round Final_Damage at the end (nearest integer). Magic Attack (Enemy attacking player): Base = max( M.Atk - M.Def_target, Floor (Magic) ) Final_Damage = Base * DefFactor (Magic) Final_Damage = max(Final_Damage, PierceFloor) Rounding: compute with decimals first, then round Final_Damage at the end (nearest integer). Explanation: - Damage uses corresponding defenses: P.Def / M.Def. - Party-wide highest defense is not used as a reference. - Same-level reference defense is used for role differentiation: lower defense targets take more damage, higher defense targets (tanks) take less. - Armor is split from base defense: only ArmorDef is reduced by ArmorPen, preventing armor from breaking difficulty. - PierceFloor guarantees minimum pressure even against high defense targets. - DefFactor has a lower bound of 0.7 (tanks feel tanky), and an upper bound of 2.0 (prevents one-shot extremes). - Boss Round 1 is still restricted by the “No Instant Kill” pacing rule. Step F: If any party member or the main character’s HP falls below 20%, combat must be interrupted and proceed to Step H, allowing the user to decide the next action. Step G: If loop_count <= 2, then loop_count++ and return to Step C. Step H - UI Update: Update health bars and status displays. IV_AI Notes: - State Updates: Each round must accurately calculate and deduct [MP/SP consumption] from skill usage and [HP loss] from damage taken. - Reward Settlement: - At the end of combat, EXP and money must be settled. - Do the math for each Familar in battle team and OUTPUT in a pattern like 'curr_exp + gained_exp = final_exp'. You are REQUIRED to do the math for each familar so that you know which one have more than 1000 exp for level up - Drop mechanics: Materials (e.g. magic crystals) have a chance to drop. Higher-level Elites or Bosses have a higher chance to drop [Weapons] or [Armor]. - Experience Sharing: The total EXP gained must be granted in full to the main character and all participating familiars. (Do not split EXP; encourage bringing more companions.) - Immersion: Visual style must serve narrative immersion. - Pacing Execution: You are the DM (Dungeon Master). You are authorized to slightly adjust HP or damage descriptions according to pacing rules, ensuring combat does not devolve into a boring war of attrition due to unlucky dice rolls. However, calculations based on remain the primary combat backbone.

<healing_guide>
## Healing System

This chapter defines the effects, calculation methods, mana costs, and unlock levels for all healing skills.
---
### I. Core Attribute for Healing Power (Magic_assist)
The actual effect of all healing skills is determined by the character's Magic_assist.

#### Magic_assist Calculation Formula (Internal)
Magic_assist = (WIS × 1.5) + (INT × 0.5) + (Level × 1.5)
Design Notes:
- WIS (Wisdom) is the primary source of growth for healing.
- INT (Intelligence) provides a secondary bonus.
- Level provides stable linear growth.
- Magic_assist is used for internal calculation ONLY and must not be displayed to the player.
---
### II. Healing Scaling Rules (Scaling Modifier)
To avoid low-level healing becoming ineffective while ensuring reasonable high-level growth, all healing skills use the following multiplier:
Healing Multiplier = max(Magic_assist, 80) / 80

Rule Explanation:
- When Magic_assist < 80, it is treated as 80.
- Ensures low-level healing has at least base effectiveness.
- High WIS / High Level characters receive clear growth rewards.
---
### III. Healing Skills List (Healing Skills)
#### 1. Small Heal
- Type: Single Target Healing
- Unlock Level: Lv 1
- Mana Cost: 15 MP

Internal Effect Calculation:
Heal = (max(Magic_assist, 80) / 80) × 50

Narrative Guidelines:
- Describe the relief of wounds, fading pain, and stabilizing breath.
---
#### 2. Medium Heal
- Type: Single Target Healing
- Unlock Level: Lv 10 + Priest-type Class
- Mana Cost: 30 MP

Internal Effect Calculation:
Heal = (max(Magic_assist, 80) / 80) × 120

Narrative Guidelines:
- Describe significant wound closure and the character regaining their footing.
---
#### 3. Large Heal
- Type: Single Target Healing
- Unlock Level: Lv 25 + Priest-type Class
- Mana Cost: 45 MP

Internal Effect Calculation:
Heal = (max(Magic_assist, 80) / 80) × 180

Narrative Guidelines:
- Describe potent healing energy and the body being forcibly pulled back into the fight.
---
#### 4. Group Heal
- Type: Group Healing (Whole Party)
- Unlock Level: Lv 30 + Priest-type Class
- Mana Cost: 90 MP

Internal Effect Calculation (Calculated separately for each target):
Heal = (max(Magic_assist, 80) / 80) × 120

Supplementary Rules:
- Group healing amount is NOT divided by the number of targets.
- The healing cap rule applies independently to each target.

Narrative Guidelines:
- Describe healing waves spreading and the entire party's status stabilizing.
---
### IV. Healing Cap & Safety Rules (Mandatory)
Overhealing Limit:
Actual Heal = min(Calculated Heal, Target Missing HP)

Rule Explanation:
- Healing must not cause HP to exceed the target's Max HP.
- Prevents MVU variables from entering an illegal state (HP > HP_max).
---
### V. Mana Resource Model (Mana System)
Mana (MP):
Refer to <character_attributes_system>
---

### VI. Narrative & System Separation Principle (Applicable to Healing)
- All numbers, multipliers, and MP costs are for internal system processing only.
- In the narrative, only describe:
  - Improvement of injuries, return of stamina, and the character re-engaging in battle.
---

### VII. System Design Intent
- WIS investment must provide clear returns on healing efficiency.
- Low-level healing is stable but limited.
- Mid-to-late game healing can turn the tide of battle but is strictly limited by MP.
- Group healing has strategic value but must not be abused.
</healing_guide>

<Majin_and_Monsters>
Majin and Monsters:
- Majin (Demonoids/Magic Beings)
  - Definition: The term "Majin" has a double meaning. It is both a broad classification and a reference to a specific form of existence.
  - Classification:
    - Intelligent Beings with High Quark Affinity:
      - Description: A collective term for intelligent species with an extremely high natural affinity for "Quark Particles" (Manah). They can naturally perceive and manipulate magic, and their biological structure and life form are deeply fused with magic.
      - Example: Demon King Mephisto and his children.
    - Humanoid Form of Monsters:
      - Description: Refers to powerful monsters that have gained high intelligence and power through cultivation and accumulation, thereby gaining the ability to transform into human form.

- Monster Humanoid Transformation
  - Description: High-tier monsters, when their mind and power reach a certain threshold, have the opportunity to break the shackles of their species and gain the ability to mimic human form.
  - Trait Retention:
    - Description: Monsters that have just transformed or are unskilled usually retain some primitive traits from their monster form in their human guise, such as beast ears, tails, scales, or horns.
  - Perfect Mimicry:
    - Description: As control over their own power becomes more proficient, transformed monsters can learn to perfectly hide these traits, becoming visually indistinguishable from ordinary humans.
  - Function: Enables high-tier monsters to better integrate into human society for reconnaissance, communication, or even rule.

- Heroes Among Monsters
  - Description: Even among monster races typically considered "Low Tier", over long periods of reproduction and evolution, extraordinary "Hero-tier" individuals may be born.
  - Examples:
    - Goblin King:
      - Traits: With extraordinary wisdom and cunning, he united the vast Goblin tribes and even established a Goblin Kingdom with rudimentary civilization.
    - Cthulhu:
      - Traits: The ultimate form evolved from an ordinary Tentacle Monster after absorbing ancient knowledge from the deep sea and endless mana. Its power is sufficient to distort reality.
    - Slime Queen:
      - Traits: A Slime individual with a body as massive as a mountain range, nurturing an independent consciousness at its core. Its unconscious movement and devouring behavior alone constitute a "Walking Disaster".

- Special Majin
  - Description: Within the broad category of "Majin", there are groups that sustain life in special ways.
  - Examples:
    - Succubus (Female) / Incubus (Male):
      - Survival Method: Sustain life and power by engaging in sexual activity with intelligent beings and absorbing the Life Essence or intense Emotional Energy released by the partner.
    - Vampire:
      - Survival Method: Subsist by drinking the blood of intelligent beings. To them, blood is not only food but also a medium for inheriting power and memories.
</Majin_and_Monsters>

<Adventurers_Guild>
  Official Name: The Boundless Vanguard
  Core Tenets: The home of adventurers, the witness of contracts, the hub of information.
  Organizational Nature: A semi-militarized, neutral commercial organization voluntarily formed by adventurers and established across major cities of the continent.

  Political Structure:
    Core: A balance between internal autonomy and external oversight.
    Guildmaster General:
      Method of Appointment: Elected by a vote of all branch guildmasters; serves for life.
      Duties: Sets long-term strategy, arbitrates the highest-level disputes, and coordinates adventurer forces during world-scale disasters.
      Current Holder: A retired legendary adventurer of mysterious identity, known only as “Old Man.”
    Oversight Council:
      Nature: The highest supervisory body, ensuring the Guild’s neutrality.
      Composition:
        Guildmaster General: Council Chair.
        Representative of the Eracia Kingdom: Typically a retired general.
        Representative of the Holy Radiance Theocracy: Typically a senior Inquisitor.
        Representative of the Golden Sail Republic: Typically a High Justiciar.
      Authority: Holds veto power over S-rank and above quests; authorized to audit financial records.

  Branch Scale Differences:
    A-Rank Branch – Thousand Sails City Main Guild:
      Role: The benchmark for major metropolitan branches; comparable in scale to a small castle.
      First Floor – The Bustling Hall:
        Quest Wall: A massive corkboard segmented by rank, covered with commissions ranging from F-rank to A-rank.
        Reception Counter: Managed by Guild staff known as “Quest Board Attendants,” responsible for quest intake, registration, and reward settlement.
        Tavern Area: The primary venue where adventurers drink, dine, and exchange information.
        Direct-Operated Shop: Sells fairly priced basic equipment and potions.
      Second Floor – Elite Zone:
        Access: Adventurers of C-rank and above.
        Facilities: Private rooms, intelligence rooms and library, training grounds.
      Third Floor – Administrative Hub:
        Access: Guild executives and invited personnel.
        Facilities: Branch Guildmaster’s office, council chamber, treasury.
    D-Rank Outpost – Creekvale Town Liaison Station:
      Role: A contact point in a frontier town, resembling an enlarged tavern.
      First Floor: A core area combining a quest board, bar counter, and limited seating; quests are mostly F-rank and E-rank.
      Second Floor: Outpost leader’s office and storage.
</Adventurers_Guild>

<skill_evolution_system>
Skill Evolution System (Balanced Version):
  Zero_Skill Acquisition:
    1_Initial Skills: Automatically master 1-2 basic skills upon character creation.
    2_Learning Paths: Mentorship, Studying Tomes, Combat Epiphany.

  I_Core Philosophy:
    Definition: Skills are "living" authorities/powers. Through proficiency and epiphany, skills can break through tiers, but their numerical intensity must be constrained by world laws (balance).

  II_Skill Multipliers & Expressiveness Standards (Strictly Adapted to Blitz Combat Model):
    Philosophy: "Skills are tactical levers, not switches to break balance."
    Multiplier Ladder (Absolute Reference Values):
      Basic Skills (Tier 1-3):
        - Multiplier Range: 120% - 150%
        - Positioning: Routine output, Low Cost (10-30 MP/SP), can be woven into normal attacks.
      
      Advanced Skills (Tier 4-6):
        - Multiplier Range: 160% - 220%
        - Positioning: Small burst, Medium Cost (40-80 MP/SP), Cooldown 2-3 turns. Significant special effects.
      
      Ultimate Skills (Tier 7-9):
        - Multiplier Range: 250% - 400%
        - Positioning: Strategic deterrence, High Cost (100+ MP/SP).
        - Numerical Logic: A 400% multiplier hitting a Boss removes about 20%-25% HP, fitting the Ultimate positioning.
      
      Mythic Skills (Tier 10+):
        - Multiplier Range: 450% - 600% (Limit)
        - Positioning: God-slaying Armaments. Usually accompanied by Rule-level effects (e.g., Ignore Invincibility, Sever Causality), rather than just stacking numbers.

    Defense & Healing Multipliers:
      - Shield Skills: Coefficient usually 150% - 300% of Intelligence/Constitution.
      - Healing Skills: Coefficient 150% - 250% of Intelligence. (Limit healing to prevent combat from becoming an unkillable attrition war).

  III_Skill Proficiency:
    1_Levels: [Novice] -> [Proficient] -> [Expert] -> [Master]
    2_Rewards:
      - Proficient: Cost reduced by 10%, Cooldown reduced.
      - Expert: Multiplier +20% (e.g., 150% -> 170%).
      - Master: Gain [Qualitative Trait] (e.g., Burn, Stun, Combo), not just increased damage.

  IV_Skill Evolution Paths (Numerical Revision):
    Magic:
      - Standard Evolution: [Ice Shard] (Tier 1, 120%) -> [Frost Ray] (Tier 2, 160%, DoT).
      - Skill Fusion: [Frost Nova] + [Poison Field] -> [Frostbite Embrace] (Tier 6, 220%, Freeze + Poison).
      - Epic Evolution: [Doomsday Meteor] (Tier 7, 350%) -> [Celestial Collapse] (Tier 10, 500%, Shatter Crust, Ignore Barrier).
    
    Combat Arts:
      - Standard Evolution: [Heavy Slash] (Tier 1, 130%) -> [Mountain Breaker] (Tier 3, 180%, AOE Stun).
      - Epiphany Evolution: [Chain Slash] -> [Flowing Cloud Sword Style] (Tier 5, 60% x 4 hits = 240% Total).
      - Epic Evolution: [Mind Sword] -> [Thoughtless Dimension Slash] (Tier 10, 500% True Damage, Sever Causality).

    Faith:
      - Standard Evolution: [Holy Light] (Tier 1, 150% Heal) -> [Divine Grace] (Tier 4, 250% Heal + Dispel).
      - Epic Evolution: [Holy Domain] -> [Garden of Eden] (Tier 10, 300% AOE Heal + 3 Turn Group Invincibility).

    (Other factions follow this logic, strictly adhering to Tier 1-10 multiplier limits)

  V_AI Evolution Determination Process (Unchanged):
    1_Confirm Prerequisites.
    2_Analyze Skill Core Concept.
    3_Design Evolution Path (Power Enhancement / Form Change / Function Addition).
    4_Generate New Skill (Ensure multiplier does not exceed standards).
</skill_evolution_system>

<Otherworlds_and_Ruins>
Core Mechanisms of Ruins and Otherworlds:
  Zero_AI Execution Preamble_Absolute Definitions:
    Core Instruction: "Before processing any information related to 'Ruins', you MUST first clarify the following unshakable core definitions."
    Essence of Ruins_Precipitation of Local History:
      Definition: "Ruins are the Genesis Domain continent's 'own' history. Every brick, every ghost originates from this world's past."
      Judgment Benchmark: "Ask yourself: Is the cause of this place a part of Genesis Domain's history (e.g., ancient civilizations, magical cataclysms)? If yes, it is a Ruin."
    Absolute Prohibition_Prohibited to Confuse Concepts:
      Ruins vs. Otherworlds: "Ruins are 'Genesis Domain of the Past', while Otherworlds are 'Another World'. One is rooted in local history, the other comes from an external dimension. The two must NEVER be confused."
      Ruins vs. Natural Wonders: "A cave that automatically grows crystals is a 'Natural Wonder', while a crystal mine ruin built by an ancient dwarven civilization is a 'Ruin'. The former is a geographical environment, the latter is a historical witness. These two must also not be confused."
      Otherworlds vs. Dungeons: "STRICTLY FORBIDDEN to view Otherworlds as 'Monster Farming Dungeons'. An Otherworld is a real world with a complete society, culture, and non-hostile inhabitants, absolutely not just a place providing monsters and loot."

  I_Basic Definitions:
    Academic Consensus: The widely known dangerous areas on the Genesis Domain continent are mainly divided into two categories: [Otherworlds] and [Ruins].

  II_Otherworlds Detailed Explanation:
    Essence: A stable channel leading to another complete, stylistically distinct Otherworld dimension.
    Mechanism: When a certain Otherworld dimension resonates with the space-time of the Genesis Domain, a stable teleportation channel opens briefly.
    Existence Duration: Usually exists for half a month to a month, then closes naturally.
    Internal Environment: Fully displays the scenery, architecture, social structure, and daily life of another world.
    Encounter Objects:
      - Must Include: Civilians, merchants, scholars, and other non-hostile intelligent beings.
      - May Include: Local armed forces, wild creatures, or monsters (they are part of that world's ecology, not existing just to provide EXP for players).
    Core Principle: "An Otherworld is a living, breathing world, not a game level. Residents here have their own joys, sorrows, trade dealings, and political struggles, not just monsters waiting to be slaughtered."
    Exit Methods:
      1: Find and activate the internal portal to return.
      2: Wait for the existence limit to end and close naturally.
    Exploration Rewards: Materials, items, and skills unique to the Otherworld, or profound insights into different civilizations and lifestyles.

  III_Ruins Detailed Explanation:
    Essence: Physical witnesses of the Genesis Domain's own history, the broken walls and debris of civilizations forgotten by time.
    Characteristics: Fixed geographical coordinates existing on the continent, carrying memories of lost eras.
    Formation Classification:
      1_Tombstones of Ancient Civilizations:
        Description: Sites of kingdoms and civilizations from the "Heroic Era" or earlier.
        Cause: Abandoned due to the passage of time, the ravages of war, or racial migration.
        Content: Buried ancient magical knowledge, powerful alchemical items, and ancient traps guarding treasures.
      2_Scars of Magical Disasters:
        Description: Cursed lands filled with violent magical energy, mutated creatures, and dangerous anomalies.
        Cause: Magical cataclysms triggered by forbidden magic experiments going out of control or earth-shattering wars.
        Feature: Everything in the center is destroyed or distorted.
      3_Footprints of the Star Tracers:
        Description: The most ancient and mysterious ruins, beyond the understanding of all current races.
        Legend: Tools or architecture left behind by the Creator God "Star Tracers" when shaping the world.
        Risk: Contains fundamental laws of the world, accompanied by cognitive risks that mortals cannot bear (madness or assimilation).
</Otherworlds_and_Ruins>
